persons.arguments_against: Strongest counter-case: Palmer is common (61 staff-list officials, 25 biographies), and the stint's listed range nominally runs to 1946 while the biography says 'ret., 1939' — could the later listings belong to a different H. R. Palmer who succeeded him? No: the stint prints the full double forename 'Herbert Richmond' with 'Sir' and K.C.M.G. in every row, the biography records exactly this appointment ('gov. and commodr.-in-ch., Cyprus, 1933' / 'gov. and c.in-c., Cyp., 1933') and the K.C.M.G. honour (dated 1933 or 1935 across versions — both before the 1934 stint start), and Governor and Commander-in-Chief of Cyprus is a singular post. The only substantive rows shown are 1934 and 1936, squarely within his 1933-1939 tenure; the 1939/1946 edition listings without rows are consistent with honoured-retiree carry-over or sparse extraction, not a second person, since no row shows anyone else's forename. Two contemporaneous 'Sir Herbert Richmond Palmer, K.C.M.G.' governors is not a realistic hypothesis.
persons.would_overturn: Evidence of a second Herbert Richmond Palmer (e.g. a son with the identical double forename and K.C.M.G.) serving in Cyprus in the 1930s, or staff-list rows after 1939 showing this name as Governor when a different governor is independently attested — which would instead indicate an extraction error, not a different person.

notes: Full double forename + 'Sir' + K.C.M.G. + the singular Cyprus governorship stated verbatim in the biography make this airtight. Minor discrepancy: the K.C.M.G. year is printed as 1935 in one bio version and 1933 in others (OCR/compositor variance); either way it precedes the stint. The stint's nominal end (1946) overshoots the bio's 'ret., 1939' — likely sparse edition coverage in the stint builder; only 1934 and 1936 rows are actually attested.
